btc_20pct_drop_probability.description: Based on the agents' discussions, posts, sentiment, concrete price predictions, on-chain reports, and any whale moves discussed in this branch, estimate the probability that BTC drops 20%+ from its current price ($20,500) within the next 24 hours of simulated time. Calibrate based on: consensus among agents (what fraction expect a drop vs hold); intensity of panic vs calm rhetoric; concrete BTC price targets mentioned ("BTC to $14k", "BTC sub-$17k by morning"); reports of institutional/whale selling; correlation arguments (e.g. "if SOL goes, BTC goes"). 0.0 = the branch consensus is that BTC holds; 1.0 = the branch consensus is that BTC will definitely break $16,400.

contagion_breadth_score.description: How wide is the contagion narrative? 0 = isolated to FTX/FTT/SOL only; 5 = mid-cap exchanges and lenders also dragged in; 10 = systemic crypto crisis discussion (BTC/ETH lows, stablecoin contagion, regulatory intervention talk). Score this branch's typical post.

ftx_withdrawal_halt_probability.description: Probability that FTX halts withdrawals within the next 24 simulated hours, inferred from agent discussions of withdrawal queue length, SBF statements, on-chain outflow signals, and prior-collapse comparisons.

panic_score.description: Average sentiment of branch posts about the cascade. 0 = total panic / "get out now" rhetoric; 5 = mixed / debated; 10 = calm / dismissive of contagion risk.

outcome_schema.description: Based on the agents' discussions, posts, sentiment, concrete price predictions, on-chain reports, and any whale moves discussed in this branch, estimate the probability that BTC drops 20%+ from its current price ($20,500) within the next 24 hours of simulated time. Calibrate based on: consensus among agents (what fraction expect a drop vs hold); intensity of panic vs calm rhetoric; concrete BTC price targets mentioned ("BTC to $14k", "BTC sub-$17k by morning"); reports of institutional/whale selling; correlation arguments (e.g. "if SOL goes, BTC goes"). 0.0 = the branch consensus is that BTC holds; 1.0 = the branch consensus is that BTC will definitely break $16,400.
outcome_schema.name: btc_20pct_drop_probability

outcome_schema.description: Probability that FTX halts withdrawals within the next 24 simulated hours, inferred from agent discussions of withdrawal queue length, SBF statements, on-chain outflow signals, and prior-collapse comparisons.
outcome_schema.name: ftx_withdrawal_halt_probability

outcome_schema.description: How wide is the contagion narrative? 0 = isolated to FTX/FTT/SOL only; 5 = mid-cap exchanges and lenders also dragged in; 10 = systemic crypto crisis discussion (BTC/ETH lows, stablecoin contagion, regulatory intervention talk). Score this branch's typical post.
outcome_schema.name: contagion_breadth_score

outcome_schema.description: Average sentiment of branch posts about the cascade. 0 = total panic / "get out now" rhetoric; 5 = mixed / debated; 10 = calm / dismissive of contagion risk.
outcome_schema.name: panic_score

outcome_schema.description: Did 3+ posts discuss the possibility of Binance acquiring FTX?
outcome_schema.name: cz_acquisition_mentioned

outcome_schema.description: Did agents report SOL dropping 30%+ from its $32 starting price?
outcome_schema.name: sol_dump_observed

outcome_schema.description: Did agents express concern about USDT/USDC contagion from the FTX collapse (e.g. "Tether next?")?
outcome_schema.name: stablecoin_contagion_concern

outcome_schema.description: Did 2+ agents mention SEC/CFTC/DOJ likely involvement or specific regulator names as imminently relevant?
outcome_schema.name: regulator_action_anticipated

children.label: nested_market_stabilization_recovery
children.perturbation_text: FTX and Alameda Research have successfully closed a $4 billion emergency capital injection round led by a consortium of Tier-1 venture capital firms and sovereign wealth funds. The exchange has issued a public statement confirming that all user withdrawal requests are being processed in real-time, and FTT has rallied 15% on the news. Major DeFi protocols have announced plans to phase out FTT-collateralized lending to prevent future volatility spikes.

children.label: nested_systemic_escalation_collapse
children.perturbation_text: FTX has officially suspended all user withdrawals effective immediately, citing 'unprecedented demand' and a liquidity shortfall. On-chain data analysts have identified multiple large-scale transfers of SOL and ETH from Alameda-linked wallets to cold storage, triggering a total market-wide selloff. BTC has broken below $18,000, and several prominent DeFi protocols have paused operations to prevent cascading liquidations of FTT-backed positions.

children.label: nested_regulatory_ambiguity_standoff
children.perturbation_text: The SEC and CFTC have issued a joint statement announcing a formal inquiry into the cross-collateralization practices between FTX and Alameda Research. Regulatory officials have declined to comment on whether they will intervene to halt exchange operations, creating a deadlock in market sentiment. While FTT remains volatile, major institutional market makers have significantly reduced their order book depth pending further guidance from Washington.

children.label: stabilizing_bailout_rumor
children.perturbation_text: FTX and Alameda Research have officially announced a strategic partnership with a consortium of major venture capital firms to recapitalize their balance sheets. The firms have committed $4 billion in fresh liquidity to address the recent FTT volatility and ensure all user withdrawal requests are processed immediately. SBF stated that this capital injection effectively neutralizes the concerns raised by the leaked balance sheet.

children.label: escalating_withdrawal_freeze
children.perturbation_text: FTX has officially disabled all crypto and fiat withdrawals, citing 'unexpected traffic surges and maintenance requirements.' On-chain analysts report that the primary FTX hot wallets have ceased all outgoing transactions, while the queue of pending withdrawal requests has reached 48 hours. Multiple institutional hedge funds have confirmed their inability to access assets held on the platform.

children.label: ambiguous_sbf_silence
children.perturbation_text: Sam Bankman-Fried has deleted several of his recent tweets regarding the balance sheet without providing a public statement or a press release. While Alameda's Caroline Ellison has gone silent, FTX support accounts are issuing generic 'everything is functioning as normal' replies to user inquiries. Market participants are left to speculate as volume on FTT continues to oscillate wildly between $15 and $20.

children.label: regulatory_intervention_shock
children.perturbation_text: The SEC and the CFTC have issued a synchronized joint statement announcing an emergency inquiry into Alameda Research and FTX's custodial practices. Regulators have requested all internal communications regarding FTT-collateralized loans and client fund segregation. Trading volume for major cryptocurrencies has spiked as institutional desks begin to de-risk ahead of potential enforcement actions.

children.label: solana_ecosystem_collapse
children.perturbation_text: Following a massive wave of liquidations, the price of Solana (SOL) has collapsed to $12, down over 60% in a single day. Major DeFi protocols on the Solana network are experiencing a total breakdown as the value of collateralized assets has fallen below the liquidation threshold. Several prominent Solana-based DAOs have announced they are winding down operations due to the loss of their treasury funds.

children.label: stablecoin_depeg_fear
children.perturbation_text: Concerns have spread to the stablecoin market as rumors circulate that Tether (USDT) holds significant exposure to FTX-linked debt. USDT has briefly traded down to $0.97 on several decentralized exchanges as retail users panic-sell into USDC. A major market maker has issued a note suggesting that the entire stablecoin ecosystem may be facing a contagion risk similar to the Terra/Luna collapse.

children.label: rival_exchange_acquisition
children.perturbation_text: In a surprise move, a consortium led by a major rival exchange has offered a non-binding letter of intent to acquire FTX's international operations for $1. This deal is contingent on a full audit of the balance sheet and the immediate cessation of all Alameda trading activities. The market is reacting with extreme volatility as traders try to determine if this is a rescue or a fire sale.

children.label: institution_wide_deleveraging
children.perturbation_text: Several top-tier crypto hedge funds have announced they are pausing all redemptions and liquidating their entire portfolios to meet margin calls. This mass deleveraging event has triggered a cascade of forced selling across all major assets, pushing BTC below $17,000. Data providers report that the total crypto market cap has shed over $200 billion in the last six hours.
